identity_constraints: Uses ordinary browser shell posture with route-level identity and publication checks.
visibility_constraints: Visibility and mutation still depend on audience coverage, release, and continuity evidence.
continuity_implications: This is the baseline shell posture for most surfaces.

channels.channel_name: Embedded webview / NHS App-style embedded channel
identity_constraints: Requires PatientEmbeddedSessionProjection, ReleaseApprovalFreeze, ChannelReleaseFreezeRecord, and bridge-capability checks.
visibility_constraints: Must preserve patient shell continuity while narrowing chrome and writable posture.
continuity_implications: Channel posture changes without changing shell ownership.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.4 Claim, secure-link, and embedded access algorithm
channels.notes: Tracked in inventory, but kept out of the current baseline per task 003 scope.

identity_constraints: Used when the shell retains continuity with reduced chrome, limited host affordances, or guarded browser handoff posture.
visibility_constraints: Artifacts, print, and export may need summary-only or recovery-only posture.
continuity_implications: The shell stays the same shell; only the channel profile changes.
channels.notes: This is the canonical shell profile used for secure-link recovery and other limited-host continuity surfaces.

identity_constraints: Phone-origin demand must converge into the same SubmissionEnvelope lineage rather than a separate back-office path.
visibility_constraints: Only public-safe capture, status, and recovery semantics apply until richer proof exists.
continuity_implications: Telephony is an ingress channel, not a shell family.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0.2 Continuity key and shell law
channels.notes: Mapped to constrained_browser shell posture only when the same patient shell must later preserve continuity on a limited host.

identity_constraints: Requires minimal grant or verified continuation before broader patient detail becomes available.
visibility_constraints: Summary-only, suppressed-recovery-only, or step-up-required visibility until the current route checks pass.
continuity_implications: Preserves one specific lineage anchor rather than opening a generic portal shell from scratch.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.4 Claim, secure-link, and embedded access algorithm

identity_constraints: Support capture uses support-shell masked scope while appending to the same patient intake lineage.
visibility_constraints: Support cannot widen subject detail beyond the current disclosure ceiling while assisting capture.
continuity_implications: This is still one governed intake lineage, not a separate support-only request system.

channels.channel_name: External delivery channels for notifications and reminders
identity_constraints: Delivery receipts do not by themselves prove patient-visible success or closure.
visibility_constraints: Preview and status must remain bounded by AudienceVisibilityCoverage and delivery evidence.
continuity_implications: Continuity-sensitive calmness must still come from authoritative settlement and evidence, not transport acceptance alone.

channels.channel_name: External delivery channels for callback and telephony outcomes
identity_constraints: Telephony provider receipts and callback outcomes must reuse the canonical effect ledger.
visibility_constraints: No callback surface may infer final truth from transport acceptance or a local dial action alone.
continuity_implications: Delivery evidence affects continuity and repair posture but does not redefine shell ownership.
